rows.notes: Page number in upper left corner. Clear printed numeral '47'.
Centered year heading '1900.' clearly written and underlined. No amounts.
Top-right title 'Balance' in large script, unambiguous. No monetary amount on this line.
Section heading 'Liabilities.' underlined; no amounts on the heading line itself.

rows.description: Cash in Money in hands of College
rows.notes: Inner set of £ s d columns (near centre of page) clearly shows '6811 10 .'. Vertical ruling lines are unambiguous; pence column blank interpreted as 0d.

rows.description: Less due to the College for Battels.
rows.notes: This line shows two numeric groups: an inner figure '2628 2 1' (the amount being deducted) and a rightmost result '4183 7 11' (net). I recorded the rightmost/final figure as the row amount because it sits in the outer £ s d columns (final balance). The word 'Battels' (college term) is legible though slightly stylised. Slight ambiguity because two amounts appear on one line; chose the outermost as the main value.
Section label introducing amounts due. Handwriting clear enough to read 'Amounts due at College:-'. No amounts on this label line.
Rightmost (outer) £ s d columns show '157 10 .'. Clear alignment with outer ruling lines; pence blank interpreted as 0d.
Major section heading 'Trust Funds:' underlined and clearly legible. No amount on the heading.
Sub-heading under Trust Funds; clearly written and introduces the list that follows. No amounts on this line.
Inner £ s d columns (near centre) show '180 14 2' aligned with the entry. All digits clear and within valid ranges.
Inner columns read '411 13 11'. Handwriting of numerals is clear; shillings and pence within valid ranges.
Inner columns show '25 15 11'. Clear digits and alignment with inner ruling lines.
Inner amount '30 11 3' is clearly written and aligned to the inner £ s d columns.
Inner set reads '86 11 4'. Clear and unambiguous.
Inner columns show '142 7 5'. Description 'Dr Richards Prize' is legible; numeric reading clear.

Inner numeric '236 15 10' is clear. The trust name is somewhat unclear in the script; read as 'Shortridge Trust (Rickany)' but handwriting of the parenthetical is difficult to decipher—the exact spelling of the trust name is uncertain.
Inner amount reads '12 8 .' (pence blank interpreted as 0d). The name of the prize is quite small and the script is ambiguous; 'Dr Sheard Prize' is an interpretive reading of the handwritten label.

Far-right (outer) total for the Trust Funds section reads '1115 12 6'. The number is boxed/underlined and aligns with the outer ruling lines. No descriptive text on the same line; recorded as a total with empty description.
Section heading 'Special Funds:' clearly underlined. No amount on this heading line.
Inner amount '868 8 1' cleanly visible in inner £ s d columns. Description 'Pension Fund' is clear.
Inner reading '62 15 10' is clear. The phrase appears as 'Dues composition Fund' in script; minor uncertainty about exact word spacing but meaning is clear.
Inner columns show '673 9 2' with clear digits and alignment. Description 'Tuition Fund' legible.
Inner amount reads '10 11 9'. Description is slightly cramped but reads as 'Undergraduates Reading Room Fund'; interpretation of exact spacing is moderately confident.
Inner amount '86 16 10' is clear. The fund name reads roughly as 'Kitchen & Buttery Fund' (handwriting makes the middle word a little uncertain — likely 'Buttery' or 'Bursary/Buttery').
Inner amount '117 1 6' is clear. The description appears to be 'Furniture House Fund' or similar; the second word is slightly unclear in the script (interpreted as 'House').
Inner columns read '29 8 10' clearly. 'Furniture Sinking Fund' is legible in the handwriting.

Small inner figure '7' visible; no shillings/pence shown explicitly (interpreted as 7 pounds 0s 0d). The ledger shows a single '7' in the inner column area; this line is faint and cramped so the exact denomination placement is moderately uncertain.

Far-right total for the Special Funds section reads '1853 12 .' — the pence column is faint/ambiguous. I recorded pence as 0d because the small digit (if any) is unclear; outer total alignment is clear but final pence digit uncertain.
Far-right figure '8696 13 2' is visible and aligns to outer columns. The reference 'II.1' is visible beside the description. Digits are clear though the script is slightly ornate.
Final grand total in lower-right corner clearly written as '£16002 15 7' in large script with underlines. Alignment with outer £ s d columns is unambiguous.
